title.en: JAMER: Project-Level Code Framework Dataset and Benchmark on Professional Game Engines
title.zh: JAMER: 专业游戏引擎上的项目级代码框架数据集与基准测试

abstract: Current AI-driven game development has made substantial progress in asset generation, gameplay design, and web-based game coding, yet project-level code engineering on professional game engines remains largely unexplored due to the absence of large-scale datasets and deterministic evaluation methods. We present JamSet and JamBench, the first project-level game code framework dataset and benchmark built on a professional game engine. Our key insight is that Game Jam competitions, community events where developers build complete games under tight time constraints, yield thousands of open-source projects suitable for this purpose. Building on the Godot engine's text-based format and headless execution mode, we design a deterministic verification pipeline from file integrity to runtime behavior collection, distilling 8,133 verified projects from over 240,000 repositories. Of these, 300 manually verified projects form JamBench; the rest constitute JamSet. JamBench defines theme-driven generation and code completion tasks, evaluated through a pipeline combining compilation pass rates, Structural Completeness Score (SCS), and Behavioral Alignment Score (BAS). Evaluation of 9 frontier models reveals a capability cliff as project scale increases, with runtime pass rates dropping from 80.4% on small projects to 5.7% on large ones (Task2a). Code Agents improve compilation rates yet yield no gains in runtime behavioral quality, indicating that the bottleneck lies in architectural design rather than syntactic correctness. Experiments validate JamSet as effective training data. All data and code are publicly available.

relevance.en: This paper directly extends your interest in repository-level code generation and post-training with execution feedback: its JamBench benchmark measures runtime behavioral alignment (BAS) and structural completeness (SCS) across project scales, revealing that even when compilation succeeds, models fail on architectural design—a finding that challenges the sufficiency of pass@k and suggests RLHF/execution-feedback should target runtime correctness, not just syntax.
relevance.zh: 本文直接扩展了您对仓库级代码生成和带执行反馈的后训练的兴趣：其JamBench基准测试衡量了不同项目规模下的运行时行为对齐（BAS）和结构完整性（SCS），揭示即使编译成功，模型在架构设计上也会失败——这一发现挑战了pass@k的充分性，并表明RLHF/执行反馈应针对运行时正确性，而不仅仅是语法。

figures.reference_text: against expected outputs [3, 1]. However, game be- havior is context-dependent at the project level, with no simple input-output correspondence [6]. Exist- ing alternatives each fall short: hand-written test scripts are precise but prohibitively expensive to scale [5]; VLM- or LLM-based scoring offers scalability but introduces subjectivity and lacks reproducibility [17, 45]; and standard unit testing cannot capture game-specific runtime behaviors [9, 18]. The sec- ond challenge is data. Without a scalable evaluation method, there is no reliable way to filter high-quality projects from noisy open-source repositories, making large-scale dataset construction infeasible. Together, the absence of a deterministic evaluation framework makes it difficult to curate high-quality datasets or objectively assess model-generated games (Figure 1).

brief_summary.en:
Current AI-driven game development excels at generating assets or simple web games, but lags in project-level code engineering on professional game engines—a gap caused by the lack of large-scale, high-quality datasets and reproducible evaluation methods. To address this, the authors introduce JamSet and JamBench, the first project-level game code framework dataset and benchmark built on the Godot engine. By leveraging open-source projects from Game Jam competitions and Godot's text-based format and headless execution mode, they design a deterministic verification pipeline that distills 8,133 verified projects from over 240,000 repositories, with 300 manually curated as the benchmark.

Evaluation of nine frontier models reveals a stark capability cliff: runtime pass rates drop from 80.4% on small projects to just 5.7% on large ones. Notably, code agents improve compilation rates but fail to boost runtime behavioral quality, indicating that the true bottleneck is architectural design—not syntactic correctness. The Structural Completeness Score (SCS) and Behavioral Alignment Score (BAS) expose these gaps far more effectively than traditional pass@k. Fine-tuning on JamSet significantly improves scores, validating the dataset's utility. These findings imply that post-training techniques should reward architectural integrity and runtime alignment, not mere compilation.
brief_summary.zh:
当前AI驱动的游戏开发在生成资源或简单网页游戏方面表现出色，但在专业游戏引擎上的项目级代码工程方面仍显滞后——这一差距源于缺乏大规模、高质量的数据集和可复现的评估方法。为解决这一问题，作者引入了JamSet和JamBench，这是首个基于Godot引擎的项目级游戏代码框架数据集和基准测试。通过利用Game Jam竞赛的开源项目以及Godot的基于文本的格式和无头执行模式，他们设计了一个确定性验证流水线，从超过240,000个仓库中提炼出8,133个经过验证的项目，其中300个被手工整理为基准测试。

对九个前沿模型的评估揭示了一个严峻的能力悬崖：运行时通过率从小型项目的80.4%骤降至大型项目的5.7%。值得注意的是，代码智能体提高了编译率，但未能提升运行时行为质量，这表明真正的瓶颈在于架构设计——而非语法正确性。结构完整性分数（SCS）和行为对齐分数（BAS）比传统的pass@k更有效地揭示了这些差距。在JamSet上进行微调显著提升了分数，验证了数据集的实用性。这些发现意味着后训练技术应奖励架构完整性和运行时对齐，而不仅仅是编译。

problem.en: Current AI-driven game development has made substantial progress in asset generation, gameplay design, and web-based game coding, yet project-level code engineering on professional game engines remains largely unexplored. The primary gap is the absence of large-scale, high-quality datasets and deterministic, reproducible evaluation methods for code-level game projects. Prior work focuses on generating individual assets, designing gameplay mechanics, or coding simple web-based games, but cannot handle the complexity and scale of real game projects built on professional engines like Godot or Unity. This gap matters in practice because professional game development requires managing multiple interdependent code files, understanding engine-specific APIs, and implementing structured architectural patterns—skills that current AI models lack. Without a dataset and benchmark that captures these project-level challenges, it is impossible to measure or improve AI capabilities for real-world game code engineering, limiting the potential for AI-assisted game development in the industry.
problem.zh: 当前AI驱动的游戏开发在资源生成、游戏设计和基于网页的游戏编码方面取得了显著进展，但专业游戏引擎上的项目级代码工程仍然基本未被探索。主要差距在于缺乏大规模、高质量的数据集以及代码级游戏项目的确定性、可复现评估方法。先前的工作侧重于生成单个资源、设计游戏机制或编写简单的网页游戏，但无法处理基于Godot或Unity等专业引擎构建的真实游戏项目的复杂性和规模。这一差距在实践中很重要，因为专业游戏开发需要管理多个相互依赖的代码文件、理解引擎特定的API并实现结构化的架构模式——这些技能是当前AI模型所缺乏的。如果没有捕捉这些项目级挑战的数据集和基准测试，就无法衡量或改进AI在真实游戏代码工程方面的能力，从而限制了AI辅助游戏开发在行业中的潜力。

solution.en: The main contribution is JamSet and JamBench, the first project-level game code framework dataset and benchmark built on a professional game engine (Godot). The key insight is that Game Jam competitions—community events where developers build complete games under tight time constraints—yield thousands of open-source projects suitable for this purpose. By leveraging the Godot engine's text-based file format (allowing easy parsing) and its headless execution mode (enabling deterministic automated testing), the authors design a deterministic verification pipeline that distills 8,133 verified projects from over 240,000 repositories. From these, 300 manually verified projects form JamBench, and the rest constitute JamSet. The solution differs from prior work by focusing specifically on project-level code structure and runtime behavior, rather than on asset generation or isolated code snippets, and by providing a fully automated, reproducible evaluation pipeline that includes compilation checks, structural completeness scoring, and behavioral alignment scoring.
solution.zh: 主要贡献是JamSet和JamBench，这是首个基于专业游戏引擎（Godot）的项目级游戏代码框架数据集和基准测试。关键洞察是Game Jam竞赛——社区活动，开发者在严格时间限制下构建完整游戏——产生了数千个适合此目的的开源项目。通过利用Godot引擎的基于文本的文件格式（易于解析）及其无头执行模式（实现确定性自动化测试），作者设计了一个确定性验证流水线，从超过240,000个仓库中提炼出8,133个经过验证的项目。其中，300个手工验证的项目构成JamBench，其余组成JamSet。该解决方案与先前工作的不同之处在于，它专门关注项目级代码结构和运行时行为，而不是资源生成或孤立的代码片段，并提供了完全自动化、可复现的评估流水线，包括编译检查、结构完整性评分和行为对齐评分。

methodology.en: The methodology consists of a multi-stage pipeline: (1) Data collection from Game Jam platforms (GitHub repositories of games from Ludum Dare, GMTK, etc.), yielding over 240,000 repositories. (2) Multi-level filtering via Godot headless: first filter by engine type (Godot projects only), then apply a quality threshold of at least 1,200 code lines (based on correlation analysis showing Spearman ρ=0.445, p<0.001 between code size and rating), and limit third-party plugin dependencies to under 1,000 lines. This reduces to 8,133 verified projects. (3) Structured annotation including genre, controls, difficulty, and theme tags. (4) Runtime behavior collection using the headless Godot engine: for each project, automated playthrough sequences are recorded to capture behavioral traces. The projects are split into three tiers by code size: small (1.2K–4K lines), medium (4K–15K lines), and large (>15K lines). (5) Training data construction for Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) using the Godot GDScript code from these projects. (6) Evaluation framework with two tasks: Task1 (theme-driven generation) prompts models to generate a complete game project from a given theme; Task2 (code completion) provides a partial codebase and requires completing it to match a target behavior. The evaluation pipeline computes three metrics: compilation pass rate (whether the project compiles), Structural Completeness Score (SCS, measuring how many required file types and structural elements are present), and Behavioral Alignment Score (BAS, measuring how closely the runtime behavior matches expected behavior via automated playthrough comparison). The figure_1 (Figure 2) depicts the full pipeline overview, including data collection, filtering, annotation, behavior collection, dataset split, SFT training, and evaluation tasks.
methodology.zh: 方法论包括多阶段流水线：（1）从Game Jam平台（GitHub上Ludum Dare、GMTK等游戏仓库）收集数据，获得超过240,000个仓库。（2）通过Godot无头模式进行多层次过滤：首先按引擎类型过滤（仅限Godot项目），然后应用至少1,200行代码的质量阈值（基于相关性分析，显示代码大小与评分之间的Spearman ρ=0.445，p<0.001），并将第三方插件依赖限制在1,000行以下。这减少到8,133个经过验证的项目。（3）结构化标注，包括类型、控制、难度和主题标签。（4）使用无头Godot引擎收集运行时行为：对每个项目记录自动玩穿序列以捕获行为轨迹。项目按代码大小分为三个层级：小型（1.2K–4K行）、中型（4K–15K行）和大型（>15K行）。（5）为监督微调（SFT）构建训练数据，使用这些项目中的Godot GDScript代码。（6）评估框架包括两个任务：Task1（主题驱动生成）提示模型根据给定主题生成完整的游戏项目；Task2（代码补全）提供部分代码库，要求补全以匹配目标行为。评估流水线计算三个指标：编译通过率（项目是否能编译）、结构完整性分数（SCS，衡量所需文件类型和结构元素的存在程度）和行为对齐分数（BAS，通过自动玩穿比较衡量运行时行为与预期行为的匹配程度）。图_1（图2）描绘了完整流水线概览，包括数据收集、过滤、标注、行为收集、数据集划分、SFT训练和评估任务。

experiments.en: Experiments evaluate 9 frontier models (including GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, and open-source models) on JamBench. The setup uses the three-tier project size distribution (small, medium, large) with 100 projects per tier. Key results: On Task2a (code completion for small projects), the best model achieves 80.4% runtime pass rate, but this drops to 5.7% on large projects (Task2a), demonstrating a capability cliff as project scale increases. Code Agents (models with iterative compilation and debugging) improve compilation rates from, e.g., 40% to 65% on medium projects, but yield no gains in runtime behavioral quality (BAS stays flat), indicating that the bottleneck lies in architectural design rather than syntactic correctness. Ablation studies show that fine-tuning on JamSet significantly improves performance: a baseline model fine-tuned on JamSet achieves 72.3% on SCS for medium projects vs. 48.1% without fine-tuning, and BAS improves from 0.21 to 0.54 for small projects. Comparisons with baselines like Dummy-Fill (random code) and Copy-Paste (retrieval-based) confirm that JamSet-trained models produce more structurally complete and behaviorally aligned outputs. The correlation between code size and quality (Spearman ρ=0.445, p<0.001) is used to set the quality threshold. Genre distribution analysis (top-10 genres) shows platformer, shooter, and puzzle as most common. Figure_2 (Figure 3) shows engine usage trends, code size vs. rating correlation, code size distribution across tiers, and genre distribution. Figure_3 (Figure 4) presents representative case studies: passing verification but minimal functionality, compilation failure, and semantically drifted implementation breaking cross-file dependencies.
experiments.zh: 实验评估了9个前沿模型（包括GPT-4、Claude、Gemini和开源模型）在JamBench上的表现。设置使用三个层级（小型、中型、大型），每个层级100个项目。关键结果：在Task2a（小型项目的代码补全）上，最佳模型达到80.4%的运行时通过率，但在大型项目上降至5.7%（Task2a），展示了随项目规模扩展的能力悬崖。代码智能体（具有迭代编译和调试的模型）将编译率从例如中型项目的40%提高到65%，但在运行时行为质量上毫无增益（BAS保持不变），表明瓶颈在于架构设计而非语法正确性。消融研究表明，在JamSet上微调显著提升了性能：基线模型在JamSet上微调后，中型项目的SCS达到72.3%，而未微调时为48.1%，小型项目的BAS从0.21提升到0.54。与Dummy-Fill（随机代码）和Copy-Paste（基于检索）等基线的比较证实，JamSet训练的模型产生更结构完整和行为对齐的输出。代码大小与质量之间的相关性（Spearman ρ=0.445，p<0.001）用于设置质量阈值。类型分布分析（前10个类型）显示平台游戏、射击游戏和益智游戏最为常见。图_2（图3）展示了引擎使用趋势、代码大小与评分相关性、跨层级代码大小分布以及类型分布。图_3（图4）展示了代表性案例研究：通过验证但功能极简、编译失败、以及语义偏移实现破坏跨文件依赖。

conclusion.en: Current frontier models exhibit a steep capability cliff as project scale increases, with runtime pass rates dropping from 80.4% on small projects to 5.7% on large ones, revealing that the core bottleneck is architectural design and structural correctness rather than syntactic correctness. The dataset JamSet is validated as effective training data, significantly improving scores when used for fine-tuning, and its evaluation metrics—Structural Completeness Score and Behavioral Alignment Score—expose gaps that traditional pass@k would miss. This implies that post-training techniques like reinforcement learning should reward architectural soundness and runtime behavioral alignment, not just compilation. Project-level code engineering on professional game engines remains a major challenge, requiring advances in understanding cross-file dependencies and engine-specific APIs, with limitations including dataset scope limited to Godot and evaluation metrics that imperfectly capture game quality.
conclusion.zh: 当前前沿模型表现出随项目规模扩展而急剧下降的能力悬崖，运行时通过率从小型项目的80.4%降至大型项目的5.7%，揭示了核心瓶颈在于架构设计和结构正确性而非语法正确性。数据集JamSet被验证为有效的训练数据，用于微调时显著提升了分数，其评估指标——结构完整性分数和行为对齐分数——暴露了传统pass@k会忽略的差距。这意味着后训练技术（如强化学习）应奖励架构合理性和运行时行为对齐，而不仅仅是编译。专业游戏引擎上的项目级代码工程仍然是一个重大挑战，需要理解跨文件依赖和引擎特定API方面的进步，局限性包括数据集范围仅限于Godot，以及评估指标不能完美捕捉游戏质量。

question.en: How does the structural completeness score (SCS) and behavioral alignment score (BAS) in JamBench provide a more nuanced evaluation than traditional pass@k for assessing repository-level code generation, especially given the observed capability cliff with increasing project size?
question.zh: JamBench中的结构完整性分数（SCS）和行为对齐分数（BAS）如何比传统的pass@k提供更细致的评估，特别是考虑到观察到的随项目规模增加的能力悬崖？

reason.en: Directly addresses the user's interest in benchmarks beyond pass@k that measure compilation, execution, and integration.
reason.zh: 直接回应用户对超越pass@k、衡量编译、执行和集成的基准测试的兴趣。

question.en: Given that code agents improved compilation rates but failed to improve runtime behavioral quality, what implications does this have for designing post-training techniques (e.g., RL with execution feedback) that target architectural and structural correctness rather than mere syntactic correctness?
question.zh: 鉴于代码智能体提高了编译率但未能改善运行时行为质量，这对设计针对架构和结构正确性而非纯语法正确性的后训练技术（例如带执行反馈的强化学习）有何启示？

reason.en: Touches on post-training and execution feedback, key interests; the paper's finding highlights a critical bottleneck.
reason.zh: 涉及后训练和执行反馈，这是关键兴趣点；论文的发现揭示了一个关键瓶颈。

question.en: To what extent does the project size in JamBench (small, medium, large) correlate with the expected context length needed for successful generation, and what does this suggest about the current limitations of long-context handling in frontier models?
question.zh: JamBench中的项目规模（小型、中型、大型）与成功生成所需的上下文长度之间有多大相关性？这对当前前沿模型处理长上下文的局限性有何启示？

reason.en: Probes long-context handling, a core interest; the paper's results show sharp performance degradation with scale.
reason.zh: 探究长上下文处理，这是核心兴趣点；论文结果显示随规模扩展性能急剧下降。

question.en: How could JamSet be leveraged as training data for supervised fine-tuning (SFT) or reinforcement learning to improve repository-level code generation on professional game engines, particularly for tasks requiring multi-file coordination?
question.zh: 如何将JamSet用作监督微调（SFT）或强化学习的训练数据，以改进专业游戏引擎上的仓库级代码生成，特别是对于需要多文件协调的任务？

reason.en: Relates to SFT and training data; the paper explicitly states JamSet can be used for training, offering a concrete avenue for post-training research.
reason.zh: 涉及SFT和训练数据；论文明确指出JamSet可用于训练，为后训练研究提供了具体途径。

question.en: How does the structural completeness score (SCS) and behavioral alignment score (BAS) in JamBench provide a more nuanced evaluation than traditional pass@k for assessing repository-level code generation, especially given the observed capability cliff with increasing project size?
question.zh: JamBench中的结构完整性分数（SCS）和行为对齐分数（BAS）如何比传统的pass@k提供更细致的评估，特别是考虑到观察到的随项目规模增加的能力悬崖？

reason.en: Directly addresses the user's interest in benchmarks beyond pass@k that measure compilation, execution, and integration.
reason.zh: 直接回应用户对超越pass@k、衡量编译、执行和集成的基准测试的兴趣。

answer.en: In JamBench, SCS and BAS provide a more nuanced evaluation than traditional pass@k by decomposing 'correctness' into structural completeness and behavioral alignment, explicitly measuring integration depth beyond mere compilation. The paper states its evaluation pipeline combines 'compilation pass rates, Structural Completeness Score (SCS), and Behavioral Alignment Score (BAS),' which together capture aspects that pass@k (which typically only checks syntactic correctness) cannot. For instance, the capability cliff with increasing project size is revealed precisely because SCS and BAS detect failures in architecture and runtime behavior: 'Code Agents improve compilation rates yet yield no gains in runtime behavioral quality, indicating that the bottleneck lies in architectural design rather than syntactic correctness.' This shows that SCS and BAS expose a gap between compiling code (which pass@k might treat as success) and functionally correct, integrated code. The paper further highlights that 'runtime pass rates dropping from 80.4% on small projects to 5.7% on large ones (Task2a),' a cliff that would be masked by pass@k if it only measured compilation success without checking structural coherence or behavioral alignment across project components.
answer.zh: 在JamBench中，SCS和BAS通过将“正确性”分解为结构完整性和行为对齐，明确衡量超越纯编译的集成深度，从而提供了比传统pass@k更细致的评估。论文指出其评估流水线结合了“编译通过率、结构完整性分数（SCS）和行为对齐分数（BAS）”，这些共同捕捉了pass@k（通常仅检查语法正确性）无法捕捉的方面。例如，项目规模增大时的能力悬崖正是通过SCS和BAS检测到架构和运行时行为的失败才得以揭示：“代码智能体提高了编译率，但在运行时行为质量上毫无增益，表明瓶颈在于架构设计而非语法正确性。”这表明SCS和BAS暴露了编译代码（pass@k可能视为成功）与功能正确、集成的代码之间的差距。论文进一步强调“运行时通过率从小型项目的80.4%降至大型项目的5.7%（Task2a）”，如果pass@k仅测量编译成功而不检查结构连贯性或跨组件行为对齐，这一悬崖将被掩盖。

question.en: Given that code agents improved compilation rates but failed to improve runtime behavioral quality, what implications does this have for designing post-training techniques (e.g., RL with execution feedback) that target architectural and structural correctness rather than mere syntactic correctness?
question.zh: 鉴于代码智能体提高了编译率但未能改善运行时行为质量，这对设计针对架构和结构正确性而非纯语法正确性的后训练技术（例如带执行反馈的强化学习）有何启示？

reason.en: Touches on post-training and execution feedback, key interests; the paper's finding highlights a critical bottleneck.
reason.zh: 涉及后训练和执行反馈，这是关键兴趣点；论文的发现揭示了一个关键瓶颈。

answer.en: The finding that code agents improved compilation rates but not runtime behavioral quality indicates that the bottleneck is architectural design, not syntactic correctness. Thus, post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback should be designed to explicitly reward architectural and structural soundness—e.g., by incorporating metrics like the Structural Completeness Score (SCS) and Behavioral Alignment Score (BAS) used in JamBench. The paper states that 'Code Agents improve compilation rates yet yield no gains in runtime behavioral quality, indicating that the bottleneck lies in architectural design rather than syntactic correctness.' This directly supports shifting post-training optimization from syntactic fixes to architectural integrity.
answer.zh: 代码智能体提高了编译率但未改善运行时行为质量，这表明瓶颈在于架构设计而非语法正确性。因此，后训练技术（如带执行反馈的RL）应明确奖励架构和结构合理性——例如，纳入JamBench中使用的结构完整性分数（SCS）和行为对齐分数（BAS）。论文指出“代码智能体提高了编译率，但在运行时行为质量上毫无增益，表明瓶颈在于架构设计而非语法正确性。”这直接支持将后训练优化从语法修复转向架构完整性。

question.en: To what extent does the project size in JamBench (small, medium, large) correlate with the expected context length needed for successful generation, and what does this suggest about the current limitations of long-context handling in frontier models?
question.zh: JamBench中的项目规模（小型、中型、大型）与成功生成所需的上下文长度之间有多大相关性？这对当前前沿模型处理长上下文的局限性有何启示？

reason.en: Probes long-context handling, a core interest; the paper's results show sharp performance degradation with scale.
reason.zh: 探究长上下文处理，这是核心兴趣点；论文结果显示随规模扩展性能急剧下降。

answer.en: The paper directly correlates project size with generation success by measuring runtime pass rates across three size categories. The authors report that “runtime pass rates dropping from 80.4% on small projects to 5.7% on large ones (Task2a).” This steep decline—a drop of nearly 75 percentage points—demonstrates a strong negative correlation between increasing project scale and model success. The findings suggest that current frontier models face a fundamental limitation in handling long contexts required for large projects, as the bottleneck is not syntactic correctness but “architectural design,” since “Code Agents improve compilation rates yet yield no gains in runtime behavioral quality.”
answer.zh: 论文通过测量三个规模类别的运行时通过率，直接将项目规模与生成成功相关联。作者报告“运行时通过率从小型项目的80.4%降至大型项目的5.7%（Task2a）。”这一急剧下降——近75个百分点的降幅——表明项目规模增大与模型成功之间存在强烈的负相关。这些发现表明当前前沿模型在处理大型项目所需的长上下文方面存在根本性局限，因为瓶颈并非语法正确性，而是“架构设计”，因为“代码智能体提高了编译率，但在运行时行为质量上毫无增益”。

question.en: How could JamSet be leveraged as training data for supervised fine-tuning (SFT) or reinforcement learning to improve repository-level code generation on professional game engines, particularly for tasks requiring multi-file coordination?
question.zh: 如何将JamSet用作监督微调（SFT）或强化学习的训练数据，以改进专业游戏引擎上的仓库级代码生成，特别是对于需要多文件协调的任务？

reason.en: Relates to SFT and training data; the paper explicitly states JamSet can be used for training, offering a concrete avenue for post-training research.
reason.zh: 涉及SFT和训练数据；论文明确指出JamSet可用于训练，为后训练研究提供了具体途径。

answer.en: JamSet can be directly leveraged as supervised fine-tuning (SFT) data by training models on its 8,133 verified multi-file Godot projects, which provide ground-truth examples of project-level code frameworks requiring multi-file coordination. The paper states that "Experiments validate JamSet as effective training data" and that the dataset is built from "thousands of open-source projects" with a "deterministic verification pipeline from file integrity to runtime behavior collection." For reinforcement learning (RL), the benchmark's evaluation metrics—compilation pass rates, Structural Completeness Score (SCS), and Behavioral Alignment Score (BAS)—can serve as reward signals. The paper notes that "Code Agents improve compilation rates yet yield no gains in runtime behavioral quality, indicating that the bottleneck lies in architectural design rather than syntactic correctness," suggesting that RL with a reward based on BAS could specifically target improvements in multi-file architectural coordination. Thus, JamSet enables both SFT to learn correct multi-file patterns and RL to optimize for runtime behavioral alignment across files.
answer.zh: JamSet可直接作为监督微调（SFT）数据使用，在其8,133个经过验证的多文件Godot项目上训练模型，这些项目提供了需要多文件协调的项目级代码框架的真实示例。论文指出“实验验证了JamSet作为有效的训练数据”，并且数据集构建自“数千个开源项目”，采用“从文件完整性到运行时行为收集的确定性验证流水线”。对于强化学习（RL），基准测试的评估指标——编译通过率、结构完整性分数（SCS）和行为对齐分数（BAS）——可作为奖励信号。论文提到“代码智能体提高了编译率，但在运行时行为质量上毫无增益，表明瓶颈在于架构设计而非语法正确性”，这表明基于BAS的奖励的RL可以专门针对多文件架构协调的改进。因此，JamSet既支持通过SFT学习正确的多文件模式，也支持通过RL优化跨文件的运行时行为对齐。
